what: Data needs for FSC-2417: 4 record type(s) required, volume=moderate, isolation=per_class_setup, sensitive=True
why: Data Need Agent mapped FSC object dependency rules to identify the minimum Salesforce records required before acceptance criteria can execute. Insertion order: 1. Individual (client) — root parent; must exist before any FSC object can be linked → 2. FinancialAccount — linked to Individual; must exist before RiskProfile__c or Suitability__c can reference it; ConsolidatedSuitabilityScore__c initialised to 0 → 3. RiskProfile__c — linked to Individual + FinancialAccount; must exist before Suitability__c is inserted so the 40% risk dimension can be resolved.

sensitive_data_fields: FinancialAccount.Balance — must use synthetic round numbers (e.g. 100000), never real AUM figures
Individual.BirthDate — must use fixed synthetic date, never real client DOB
Individual.FirstName / LastName — must use clearly synthetic test-only names (e.g. 'Test Client_Synthetic')
RiskProfile__c.CapacityForLoss__c — must use synthetic classification values, not derived from real client data
Suitability__c.RiskProfileScore__c / InvestmentObjectivesScore__c / FinancialCircumstancesScore__c — must use synthetic integer scores, never real assessment outputs
FinancialAccount.ConsolidatedSuitabilityScore__c — must be seeded with synthetic values (e.g. 0) and validated against known synthetic inputs
factory_classes_recommended: SuitabilityTestDataFactory.createIndividualClient() — creates a synthetic Individual with masked PII fields
SuitabilityTestDataFactory.createFinancialAccount(Individual clientId) — creates a FinancialAccount linked to an Individual with ConsolidatedSuitabilityScore__c initialised to 0
SuitabilityTestDataFactory.createRiskProfile(Id financialAccountId) — creates a RiskProfile__c with synthetic tolerance/horizon/capacity values

SuitabilityTestDataFactory.createSuitabilityRecord(Id financialAccountId, Decimal riskScore, Decimal objectivesScore, Decimal circumstancesScore) — creates a Suitability__c record with all three dimension scores set to known synthetic values
SuitabilityTestDataFactory.createFullSuitabilityChain() — convenience method composing all of the above in correct dependency order; returns a map of all created records
SuitabilityTestDataFactory.createStaleSuitabilityRecord(Id financialAccountId) — creates a Suitability__c with a LastAssessedDate__c in the past to exercise stale-data alert logic
data_dependencies_ordered: 1. Individual (client) — root parent; must exist before any FSC object can be linked
2. FinancialAccount — linked to Individual; must exist before RiskProfile__c or Suitability__c can reference it; ConsolidatedSuitabilityScore__c initialised to 0
3. RiskProfile__c — linked to Individual + FinancialAccount; must exist before Suitability__c is inserted so the 40% risk dimension can be resolved

4. Suitability__c — linked to FinancialAccount (and via relationship to RiskProfile__c); triggering an insert/update fires SuitabilityScoreCalculator and should recalculate ConsolidatedSuitabilityScore__c on FinancialAccount
risks: Suitability__c requires a correctly linked RiskProfile__c before insert — if RiskProfile__c is missing or inserted after Suitability__c, SuitabilityScoreCalculator will fail to resolve the 40% risk dimension and return an incorrect or null score

ConsolidatedSuitabilityScore__c on FinancialAccount must be initialised to 0 in test setup; if left null, numeric assertion comparisons in score calculation tests may throw unexpected NullPointerException

The SuitabilityScoreCalculator Apex trigger is fired by Suitability__c update — @TestSetup data must not update Suitability__c records after creation, or the trigger will fire during setup and pollute the baseline score before the test begins

Per-class setup (@TestSetup) runs once and data is shared across all test methods in the class — any test that modifies FinancialAccount.ConsolidatedSuitabilityScore__c directly must use Test.startTest()/stopTest() boundaries and avoid leaving dirty state that affects score-assertion tests

Platform Event SuitabilityScoreUpdated__e cannot be published within a @TestSetup context — tests covering real-time dashboard refresh via the Platform Event must use per_test_setup_teardown or mock the event publication using a Test.enableChangeDataCapture() pattern

Stale-data alert tests require a Suitability__c record with a controlled LastAssessedDate__c in the past — this variant must be created separately from the 'active' baseline records to avoid date-dependency failures when tests run at month or year boundaries

Bulk recalculation tests (if included for governor limit coverage) must use at least 200 Suitability__c records linked to distinct FinancialAccounts — ensure the factory method supports bulk insert mode to avoid hitting DML row limits during test setup
Financial amounts (Balance, AUM) must use synthetic round numbers to prevent assertions becoming brittle if business rounding logic changes
